audit_name: Track A6: crucix + ws-gateway Hand-Roll Audit
crucix_purpose: Crucix is a local OSINT intelligence engine that aggregates 26+ data sources, computes signal deltas across sweep cycles, evaluates alerts via LLM with tiered urgency, and provides Telegram/Discord bot interfaces for remote control.

hand_roll_candidates.description: Custom state and delta tracking system for storing sweep cycle outputs and computing changes over time. Maintains persistent state in runs/ directory with manual pruning logic.
hand_roll_candidates.justification: This is a genuine candidate—delta computation and sweep-cycle state management could be delegated to a battle-tested persistence + queue library. Domain logic (OSINT delta semantics) is thin; infrastructure (storage, pruning, versioning) is standard.

hand_roll_candidates.description: Custom abstraction layer providing unified interface to 9 LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Gemini, Codex, MiniMax, Mistral, Ollama, Grok). Factory function handles provider selection and initialization.
hand_roll_candidates.justification: Custom LLM factory is a genuine candidate. The problem (provider abstraction) is solved well by langchain + litellm. Current implementation duplicates this work. Domain logic is thin (provider selection); infrastructure (API calls, error handling, response parsing) is standard.

hand_roll_candidates.description: 768-line custom Telegram bot implementation with: polling API (5s interval, offset tracking), tiered alert system (FLASH/PRIORITY/ROUTINE with cooldowns), semantic deduplication (content hashing, 4h window), rate limiting, LLM evaluation with sophisticated prompt, rule-based fallback, two-way bot commands, message chunking (4096 char limit), bot identity management, Markdown formatting.
hand_roll_candidates.current_implementation: Custom Telegram polling bot with built-in alert orchestration
library_alternatives: telegraf.js (Telegram bot framework, handles polling + webhooks)
hand_roll_candidates.justification: This is a high-severity candidate. telegraf.js + opsgenie-node would eliminate 700+ lines of custom code. Tiered alert logic (FLASH/PRIORITY/ROUTINE) is domain-specific and worth keeping, but polling infrastructure, command dispatch, and rate limiting are standard. Current implementation couples domain (alert tiers) with infrastructure (Telegram polling). Split into telegraf.js (infrastructure) + custom alert evaluator (domain).

hand_roll_candidates.description: Custom Discord bot integration (file exists, not fully audited; assumed similar pattern to Telegram with Discord.js as optional dependency in package.json).
hand_roll_candidates.current_implementation: Custom Discord bot (discord.js in optional deps)
library_alternatives: discord.js (already listed as optional dep—use directly)
hand_roll_candidates.justification: discord.js is already in package.json as optional dep. If custom implementation exists, it should be removed in favor of discord.js directly. Domain logic (alert tiers, evaluation) can wrap discord.js without reimplementation.

hand_roll_candidates.description: Custom dashboard synthesis pipeline (file exists, not fully audited; transforms OSINT data into dashboard format for browser clients via SSE).
hand_roll_candidates.justification: Dashboard logic depends on domain (OSINT data shape, geopolitical signals). If inject.mjs is pure data transformation (normalization, aggregation), candidate for extraction into schema-based transform library. If it includes visualization, investigate whether recharts or vega-lite would reduce DOM manipulation code.

hand_roll_candidates.description: Manual Set<Response> management for Server-Sent Events broadcasting to multiple browser clients. Implements subscribe/broadcast pattern directly.
hand_roll_candidates.current_implementation: Manual response tracking in Set + write(chunk) calls
library_alternatives: socket.io (already used in ws-gateway, not in crucix)
hand_roll_candidates.justification: Low priority—current SSE implementation is simple and correct. However, if real-time updates are critical, migrating to Socket.IO (used in ws-gateway) would provide fallback transports (polling, WebSocket) and unified client library. Candidate only if scaling real-time reliability matters.

ws_gateway_audit.note: ws-gateway correctly outsources all infrastructure to battle-tested libraries (nats@^2.28.2, socket.io@^4.8.1). No hand-roll candidates detected. Proper retry logic, error handling, graceful shutdown. Architecture is minimal and well-executed.

summary.ws_gateway_assessment: No hand-rolls detected—clean use of standard libraries
